title: Contributing to AISanalyze
text: aim AISanalyze provide reliable reproducible tools analysing Automatic Identification System (AIS) data. Contributions improving performance, documentation, testing, functionality welcome, including: Reporting bugs. Suggesting new features improvements. Improving documentation examples. Submitting pull requests.

title: Contributing to AISanalyze
text: Please use GitHub Issues page report bugs request new features. reporting bug, please include: version AISanalyze; R version (sessionInfo()); minimal reproducible example; complete error message, applicable.

title: Contributing to AISanalyze
text: submitting pull request, please: follow existing coding style; document new exported functions using roxygen2; add update unit tests appropriate; ensure devtools::check() completes without errors.

title: Contributing to AISanalyze
text: questions using AISanalyze, please contact remi.pigeault@gmail.com open GitHub Discussion Issue. Thank helping improve AISanalyze!

title: AISanalyze: User guide
text: AISanalyze provides workflow analyse Automatic Identification System (AIS) data, including: estimating vessel travel distance, time speed; correcting GPS errors delays; identifying AIS stations aircraft; interpolating vessel positions; extracting vessels around target locations; estimating vessel characteristics. vignette illustrates typical workflow.

title: AISanalyze: User guide
text: Convert timestamps Unix time.
code: data("ais") data("point_to_extract") ais$timestamp <- as.numeric(ymd_hms(ais$datetime)) point_to_extract$timestamp <- as.numeric(ymd_hm(point_to_extract$datetime))

title: AISanalyze: User guide
text: Three variables added: distance_travelled time_travelled speed_kmh
code: ais <- AIStravel(ais_data = ais)

title: AISanalyze: User guide
text: Two logical variables added: station high_speed
code: ais <- AISidentify_stations_aircraft(ais_data = ais) #> Stations and aircraft are identified from speed, distance and time only. Other criteria (e.g. MMSIs with fewer than 9 digits) are not considered.

title: AISanalyze: User guide
text: step corrects unrealistic speeds caused GPS errors transmission delays.
code: ais <- AIScorrect_speed(ais_data = ais) #> For consecutive GPS errors, only the first point is removed to avoid overcorrection. #> High-speed craft are not corrected.

title: AISanalyze: User guide
text: example interpolates vessel positions every 60 seconds. datetime column can updated interpolated timestamps: Alternatively, interpolation can performed exact timestamps. Target locations search radius (m) can specified limit interpolation area interest reduce computation time. datetime column can updated new timestamp.
code: ais_interpolated_60sec <- AISinterpolate(   ais_data = ais,   type_interpolation = "maximum_time_interval",   maximum_time_interval = list(     maximum_gap_seconds = 60   ) ) ais_interpolated_60sec$datetime <- lubridate::as_datetime(ais_interpolated_60sec$timestamp) ais_interpolated_exact_timestamps <- AISinterpolate(   ais_data = ais,   type_interpolation = "exact_timestamp",   exact_timestamp = list(     timestamp_to_interpolate = point_to_extract$timestamp,     locations_of_interest = point_to_extract[c("lon", "lat")],     radius = 200000   ) )  ais_interpolated_exact_timestamps$datetime <- lubridate::as_datetime(ais_interpolated_exact_timestamps$timestamp)

title: AISanalyze: User guide
text: Extract vessel positions within 50 km ±5 minutes target locations timestamps (point_to_extract). Set return_all_vessel_locations = FALSE return vessel position target timestamps:
code: AISextract(   data = point_to_extract,   ais_data = ais_interpolated_60sec,   return_all_vessel_locations = TRUE,   search_into_radius_m = 50000,   interval_time_before = 300,   interval_time_after = 300 ) AISextract(   data = point_to_extract,   ais_data = ais_interpolated_exact_timestamps,   return_all_vessel_locations = FALSE,   search_into_radius_m = 50000,   interval_time_before = 300,   interval_time_after = 300 )

title: AISanalyze: User guide
text: function estimates likely vessel characteristics MMSI, including ship type, dimensions, draught, IMO number, name. summary_values summarises values found AIS data, whereas estimated_values contains estimated characteristic vessel.
code: infos <- AISinfos(ais)  summary_values <- infos$summary estimated_values <- infos$estimated_values

title: AISanalyze: User guide
text: recommended workflow :
code: AIS data │ ▼ AIStravel() │ ▼ AISidentify_stations_aircraft() (optional) │ ▼ AIScorrect_speed() (optional) │ ▼ AISinterpolate()   (optional) │ ▼ AISextract() │ ▼ AISinfos()         (optional)

title: AISanalyze
text: complete step--step workflow available User guide. 📖 Full documentation, tutorials function reference: https://remip48.github.io/AISanalyze

title: AISanalyze
text: AISanalyze R package analysing Automatic Identification System (AIS) data. provides tools estimate vessel travel distance, time speed; correct GPS errors delays; identify AIS base stations aircraft; interpolate vessel positions; extract vessel positions around target locations times; estimate vessel characteristics (ship type, length, width, draught, IMO name).

title: AISanalyze
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("remip48/AISanalyze")

title: AISanalyze
code: library(AISanalyze)  data("ais") data("point_to_extract")  ## define the Unix time (seconds since 1970-01-01) ais$timestamp <- as.numeric(lubridate::ymd_hms(ais$datetime))) point_to_extract$timestamp <- as.numeric(lubridate::ymd_hm(point_to_extract$datetime)))  ## process, correct, interpolate and extract AIS data: ais <- AIStravel(ais)  ais_identified <- AISidentify_stations_aircraft(ais)  ais_corrected <- AIScorrect_speed(ais_identified)  ais_interpolated <- AISinterpolate(ais_data = ais_corrected,                                    type_interpolation = "maximum_time_interval",                                    maximum_time_interval = list(maximum_gap_seconds = 60))  ais_extracted <- AISextract(ais_data = ais_interpolated,                             data = point_to_extract,                             return_all_vessel_locations = T,                             search_into_radius_m = 50000)  head(ais_extracted)

title: AISanalyze
text: use AISanalyze, please cite: Pigeault R., Ruser ., Ramírez-Martínez N.C., Geelhoed S.C.V., Haelters J., Nachtsheim D.., Schaffeld T., Sveegaard S., Siebert U., Gilles . (2024). Maritime traffic alters distribution harbour porpoise North Sea. Marine Pollution Bulletin. 208: 116925.

title: AISanalyze
text: project licensed MIT License. See LICENSE file details.

title: Summary
text: AISanalyze R-package developed specifically correct GPS errors AIS data (Automatic Identification System data), linearly interpolate vessel positions desired times, extract locations information vessels around desired locations/times. advantage package computation time considerably reduced perform operations, enabling results obtained minutes hours depending size AIS data number points extract. package distinguishes vessels base-stations aircrafts AIS data (based distance speed travelled), allowing users remove latter needed. package also extracts information vessel length type, excluding errors originally present AIS data. First, --one function constructed analyse, correct, extract AIS data around desired locations, therefore: calculating distance, time speed travelled vessel; identifying base stations aircrafts AIS; correcting GPS errors GPS delays; interpolating vessel positions customized times; extracting vessel positions information around desired locations/times. However, function also available individually. Second, function allows linearly interpolate AIS data desired temporal resolution, regardless location. Third, last function extracts length type vessel per MMSI (Maritime Mobile Service Identity), removing errors present AIS data. information can later added interpolated/extracted AIS data.

title: Summary
text: Human activities impacts ocean ecosystem components continue increase (Halpern et al., 2008, 2015), including maritime traffic. AIS (Automatic Identification System) VMS data (Vessel Monitoring System) sources global data vessel positions real time date. Studies impacts maritime traffic therefore heavily dependent data: however, numerous errors found, due GPS errors, GPS delays, errors receptions. leads vessel tracks erroneous positions, speeds, information (e.g., wrong vessel length type). huge size AIS data makes overview complex highly time-consuming. two points greatly limit research possibilities related vessel tracks densities wider context. Easy--use fast calculation algorithms required fill gaps allow community use AIS data carry researches. AISanalyze opens wide range research possibilities, including study maritime traffic effects distribution improvement AIS data shortcomings processing (e.g. Pigeault et al., 2024).

title: Summary
text: acknowledge contributions Daniel Alexander Schwarzkopf, Volker Matthias, Tobias Schaffeld, Dominik Nachtsheim. like thank German Federal Maritime Hydrographic Agency (BSH) providing AIS data enabled us build, check use package. work conducted framework CoastalFutures project, funded German Federal Ministry Education Research (BMBF) grant number 03F0911H. responsibility content publication lies authors.

title: AISanalyze — AISanalyze
text: Tools analysing, cleaning, interpolating extracting Automatic Identification System (AIS) vessel data.

title: AISanalyze — AISanalyze
text: Travel metrics AIStravel() AIScorrect_speed() Vessel characteristics AISidentify_stations_aircraft() AISinfos() Interpolation AISinterpolate() Extraction AISextract()

title: AISanalyze — AISanalyze
text: Maintainer: R<U+00E9>mi Pigeault remi.pigeault@tiho-hannover.de Authors: R<U+00E9>mi Pigeault remi.pigeault@tiho-hannover.de

what: Correct GPS errors in AIS tracks — AIScorrect_speed
title: Correct GPS errors in AIS tracks — AIScorrect_speed
text: Detects corrects GPS errors delayed AIS messages generate unrealistic vessel speeds, travelled distances, travel times.

title: Correct GPS errors in AIS tracks — AIScorrect_speed
code: AIScorrect_speed(   ais_data,   crs_meters = 3035,   threshold_speed_to_correct = 100,   threshold_speed_to_correct_expr = function(speed_kmh) {      return(15 +     stats::median(speed_kmh[speed_kmh > 1], na.rm = T) + 5 *     stats::sd(speed_kmh[speed_kmh > 1 & speed_kmh < stats::quantile(speed_kmh[speed_kmh >     1], 0.75)]))  } )

title: Correct GPS errors in AIS tracks — AIScorrect_speed
text: ais_data AIS data frame containing timestamp, lon, lat, mmsi. timestamp must Unix time (seconds since 1970-01-01), lon lat must numeric. Another vessel identifier may used column named mmsi. crs_meters CRS (metres) used calculate travelled distances. Defaults EPSG:3035. threshold_speed_to_correct Speed threshold (km/h) observations corrected. threshold_speed_to_correct_expr Function returning vessel-specific speed threshold speed_kmh.

title: Correct GPS errors in AIS tracks — AIScorrect_speed
text: input data corrected travel metrics following columns: speed_kmh_corrected: Whether speed corrected. time_travelled: Travel time (s). distance_travelled: Travelled distance (m). speed_kmh: Vessel speed (km/h).

title: Correct GPS errors in AIS tracks — AIScorrect_speed
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(AISanalyze) data("ais")  ais <- ais %>%   mutate(timestamp = as.numeric(ymd_hms(datetime))) %>%   AIStravel(ais_data = .)  out <- AIScorrect_speed(ais_data = ais,                  crs_meters = 3035,                  threshold_speed_to_correct = 100)} # }

what: Extract AIS positions around target locations and times — AISextract
title: Extract AIS positions around target locations and times — AISextract
text: Returns either (depending return_all_vessel_locations): vessel position target timestamps. vessel positions within specified time window.

title: Extract AIS positions around target locations and times — AISextract
code: AISextract(   data,   ais_data,   crs_meters = 3035,   return_all_vessel_locations = T,   search_into_radius_m = 50000,   interval_time_before = 5 * 60,   interval_time_after = 5 * 60 )

title: Extract AIS positions around target locations and times — AISextract
text: data Data frame containing timestamp, lon, lat. timestamp must Unix time (seconds since 1970-01-01), lon lat must numeric. ais_data AIS data frame containing timestamp, lon, lat, mmsi. timestamp, lon, lat must numeric. Another vessel identifier may used column named mmsi. crs_meters CRS (metres) used calculate distances. Defaults EPSG:3035. return_all_vessel_locations Logical. TRUE, returns vessel positions within specified time window. Otherwise, returns closest position time. search_into_radius_m Search radius (m). interval_time_before Time window (s) data$timestamp. interval_time_after Time window (s) data$timestamp.

title: Extract AIS positions around target locations and times — AISextract
text: data joined matching AIS positions. Rows duplicated several vessel positions match target location time. vessel found, AIS columns (including mmsi) filled NA. output also includes distance_vessel_to_location_m, distance (m) target location vessel position.

title: Extract AIS positions around target locations and times — AISextract
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(AISanalyze) data("ais") data("point_to_extract")  point_to_extract <- point_to_extract %>%   mutate(timestamp = as.numeric(ymd_hm(datetime)))  ais <- ais %>%   mutate(timestamp = as.numeric(ymd_hms(datetime))) %>%   AIStravel(ais_data = .) %>%   AISinterpolate(ais_data = .,            type_interpolation = "exact_timestamp",            exact_timestamp = list(              timestamp_to_interpolate = point_to_extract$timestamp,              locations_of_interest = data.frame(lon = point_to_extract$lon,                                                 lat = point_to_extract$lat),              radius = 200000),            crs_meters = 3035)  # to return all vessel positions around the target location/timestamps: out <- AISextract(data = point_to_extract,            ais_data = ais,            crs_meters = 3035,            return_all_vessel_locations = TRUE,            search_into_radius_m = 50000,            interval_time_before = 5 * 60,            interval_time_after = 5 * 60)  # to return the position of each vessel closest in time to the target # timestamps (around the target location) out <- AISextract(data = point_to_extract,            ais_data = ais,            crs_meters = 3035,            return_all_vessel_locations = FALSE,            search_into_radius_m = 50000,            interval_time_before = 5 * 60,            interval_time_after = 5 * 60)            } # }

what: Identify AIS base stations and high-speed craft — AISidentify_stations_aircraft
title: Identify AIS base stations and high-speed craft — AISidentify_stations_aircraft
text: Identify AIS base stations high-speed craft

title: Identify AIS base stations and high-speed craft — AISidentify_stations_aircraft
code: AISidentify_stations_aircraft(ais_data, crs_meters = 3035)

title: Identify AIS base stations and high-speed craft — AISidentify_stations_aircraft
text: ais_data AIS data frame containing timestamp, lon, lat, mmsi. timestamp must Unix time (seconds since 1970-01-01), lon lat must numeric. Another vessel identifier may used column named mmsi. crs_meters CRS (metres) used calculate distances. Defaults EPSG:3035.

title: Identify AIS base stations and high-speed craft — AISidentify_stations_aircraft
text: input AIS data following additional columns: station: Whether MMSI classified base station. high_speed: Whether MMSI classified high-speed craft. n_point_mmsi_initial_data: Number valid AIS positions MMSI. id_mmsi_point_initial: Sequential identifier AIS position.

title: Identify AIS base stations and high-speed craft — AISidentify_stations_aircraft
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(AISanalyze) data("ais")  ais <- ais %>%   mutate(timestamp = as.numeric(ymd_hms(datetime))) %>%   AIStravel(.)  out <- AISidentify_stations_aircraft(ais_data = ais)} # }

title: Estimate vessel characteristics — AISinfos
text: Estimates likely vessel characteristics mmsi AIS messages, including ship type, length, width, draught, IMO number, vessel name. Estimates based frequent values, giving greater weight records complete information.

title: Estimate vessel characteristics — AISinfos
code: AISinfos(   ais_data,   threshold_length = 475,   threshold_draught = 30,   threshold_width = 75,   weight_complete_data = 10 )

title: Estimate vessel characteristics — AISinfos
text: ais_data AIS data frame containing columns mmsi, shiptype, length, width, draught, imo, name. Another vessel identifier may used column named mmsi. threshold_length Maximum valid vessel length (m). Larger values set NA. threshold_draught Maximum valid draught (m). Larger values set NA. threshold_width Maximum valid vessel width (m). Larger values set NA. weight_complete_data Weight assigned records containing vessel length ship type.

title: Estimate vessel characteristics — AISinfos
text: list containing: estimated_values: Estimated vessel characteristics mmsi. summary: Summary statistics mmsi, including: Number AIS positions. Number non-missing values length, shiptype, width, draught, imo, name. valid values observed characteristic. likely value characteristic.

title: Estimate vessel characteristics — AISinfos
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(AISanalyze) data("ais")  out <- AISinfos(ais_data = ais)} # }

title: Interpolate AIS positions — AISinterpolate
text: Interpolates vessel positions either: (depending type_interpolation) ensure time intervals exceed specified maximum (maximum_time_interval). user-defined timestamps (exact_timestamp). Interpolation can optionally restricted given radius within target locations reduce computation time.

title: Interpolate AIS positions — AISinterpolate
code: AISinterpolate(   ais_data,   type_interpolation,   maximum_time_interval = list(maximum_gap_seconds = 10 * 60),   exact_timestamp = list(timestamp_to_interpolate = NULL, locations_of_interest = NULL,     radius = 2e+05),   crs_meters = 3035,   nb_cores = 1,   outfile = "log.txt" )

title: Interpolate AIS positions — AISinterpolate
text: ais_data AIS data frame containing timestamp, lon, lat, mmsi. timestamp must Unix time (seconds since 1970-01-01), lon lat must numeric. type_interpolation Interpolation mode: "maximum_time_interval" "exact_timestamp". maximum_time_interval List used type_interpolation = "maximum_time_interval", containing maximum_gap_seconds. exact_timestamp List used type_interpolation = "exact_timestamp", containing: timestamp_to_interpolate: Target timestamps. locations_of_interest: Optional data frame lon lat columns corresponding timestamp_to_interpolate. radius: Optional search radius (m) around target location. crs_meters CRS (metres) used distance calculations. Defaults EPSG:3035. nb_cores Number CPU cores used type_interpolation = exact_timestamp. outfile File used save logs type_interpolation = exact_timestamp.

title: Interpolate AIS positions — AISinterpolate
text: interpolated AIS data additional column: interpolated: Whether position interpolated.

title: Interpolate AIS positions — AISinterpolate
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(AISanalyze) data("ais") data("point_to_extract")  point_to_extract <- point_to_extract %>%   mutate(timestamp = as.numeric(ymd_hm(datetime)))  ais <- ais %>%   mutate(timestamp = as.numeric(ymd_hms(datetime))) %>%   AIStravel(ais_data = .)  # to interpolate all vessel locations separated by > 60 seconds out <- AISinterpolate(ais_data = ais,                type_interpolation = "maximum_time_interval",                maximum_time_interval = list(maximum_gap_seconds = 60),                crs_meters = 3035)  # to interpolate all vessel locations at exact timestamps, # within a radius of 200 000 meters around # target locations out <- AISinterpolate(ais_data = ais,            type_interpolation = "exact_timestamp",            exact_timestamp = list(                timestamp_to_interpolate = point_to_extract$timestamp,                locations_of_interest = data.frame(lon = point_to_extract$lon,                                                  lat = point_to_extract$lat),                radius = 200000),            crs_meters = 3035)            } # }

title: Calculate vessel travel metrics — AIStravel
text: Calculates distance travelled (m), travel time (s), speed (km/h) consecutive AIS positions vessel (mmsi).

title: Calculate vessel travel metrics — AIStravel
code: AIStravel(ais_data, crs_meters = 3035)

title: Calculate vessel travel metrics — AIStravel
text: ais_data AIS data frame containing timestamp, lon, lat, mmsi. timestamp must Unix time (seconds since 1970-01-01), lon lat must numeric. Another vessel identifier may used column named mmsi. crs_meters CRS (metres) used calculate distances. Defaults EPSG:3035.

title: Calculate vessel travel metrics — AIStravel
text: input AIS data following additional columns: time_travelled: Travel time (s) since previous AIS position. distance_travelled: Distance travelled (m) since previous AIS position. speed_kmh: Vessel speed (km/h). X, Y: coordinates crs_meters.

title: Calculate vessel travel metrics — AIStravel
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(AISanalyze) data("ais")  ais <- ais %>%   mutate(timestamp = as.numeric(ymd_hms(datetime)))  out <- AIStravel(ais_data = ais)} # }

title: Example AIS dataset — ais
text: subset Automatic Identification System (AIS) messages collected North Sea 1 November 2022. dataset contains vessel positions together static dynamic AIS information, including vessel identity, navigational status, speed, heading, dimensions, draught destination.

title: Example AIS dataset — ais
text: data frame AIS messages multiple vessels. main variables include: datetime Date time AIS message (UTC). mmsi Anonymized Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) vessel. lon Longitude (decimal degrees, WGS84). lat Latitude (decimal degrees, WGS84). navigational_status Reported navigational status. SOG Speed ground (knots). Heading True heading (degrees). imo Reported International Maritime Organization (IMO) number. shiptype Reported vessel type. length Reported vessel length (m). name Reported vessel name. width Reported vessel width (m). draught Reported vessel draught (m).

title: Example AIS dataset — ais
text: Example subset extracted AIS observations.

title: Example AIS dataset — ais
text: dataset intended demonstrating main functions AISanalyze, including travel distance estimation, GPS error correction, vessel characteristic estimation, interpolation vessel extraction. Original MMSI identifiers replaced anonymized values protect vessel confidentiality.

title: Example extraction locations — point_to_extract
text: Example locations timestamps used demonstrate AISextract() interpolation exact timestamps AISinterpolate(). row defines target location time nearby vessel positions can extracted interpolated.

title: Example extraction locations — point_to_extract
text: data frame following variables: point Unique identifier target location. lon, lat Longitude latitude (WGS84). datetime Target date time (UTC).

title: AISanalyze 2.0.0 (2026-07-20)
text: Added comprehensive unit tests. Added package vignette complete workflow. Added package help page providing overview public API. Added pkgdown website online documentation.

title: AISanalyze 2.0.0 (2026-07-20)
text: Refactored large functions smaller helper functions improved maintainability. Simplified package workflow. Improved expanded function documentation examples. Added continuous integration GitHub Actions. Added NEWS.md CONTRIBUTING.md.

title: AISanalyze 2.0.0 (2026-07-20)
text: Fixed vessel extraction single AIS position available. Improved handling edge cases throughout package.

title: AISanalyze 1.1.0 (2026-02-10)
text: First public release GitHub. Added accompanying manuscript.

title: AISanalyze 1.1.0 (2026-02-10)
text: Improved function documentation.

title: AISanalyze 1.1.0 (2026-02-10)
text: Fixed AIScorrect_speed() stationary vessels. Corrected time_travelled distance_travelled consecutive interpolated positions.

title: AISanalyze 1.0.0 (2025-06-17)
